summary: Wall Street closed higher and European equities rallied broadly as optimism around a potential US-Iran peace deal lifted risk sentiment, with Pakistan's Prime Minister confirming the final text has been agreed and a US official stating a signing could come within days. The Iran nuclear/conflict resolution talks stand as the dominant macro catalyst, with the UAE reportedly set to unlock billions of dollars for Iran upon agreement, while Trump simultaneously disputed leaked deal terms, injecting residual uncertainty into an otherwise constructive narrative. SpaceX's historic market debut was the standout corporate event, generating a significant payday for Goldman Sachs on the IPO and drawing intense scrutiny over whether the company can deliver on its ambitious moonshot targets to justify its valuation, while FedEx's ongoing turnaround faces a critical near-term test. Oil markets moved in the opposite direction to equities, with Brent crude falling to its lowest level since March on expectations that a deal would ease geopolitical supply risk, and Bitcoin's renewed volatility reignited debate about its role as a portfolio asset. In the short term, markets will remain highly sensitive to confirmation or collapse of the Iran deal, with any signed agreement likely to sustain equity gains and further pressure crude prices, while a breakdown in negotiations or contradictory statements from Washington could quickly reverse the week's risk-on momentum.
